steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts resin (effective but slow, due to long curing time) with plastic sheets, which match resin's stabilizing utility while allowing near-instantaneous embedding. The question asks what conclusion is best supported by this speed-with-quality advantage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'maintains established qualities': frames plastic as merely preserving the status quo rather than introducing a speed improvement. ✗
  • Choice B — 'enhance the efficiency of preparatory steps before sectioning without compromising cross-sectional shape': directly captures the speed advantage (efficiency) paired with maintained quality (no compromise to shape). ✓
  • Choice C — role of a stabilizing agent': contradicts the passage, which confirms plastic does act as a stabilizer. ✗
  • Choice D — 'maintains established qualities': like A, treats the outcome as preserving rather than improving efficiency. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
